title: Structured outputs vs prompt-based JSON extraction

body.itemA: Structured outputs (output_config.format or strict tool use)
body.itemB: Prompt-based JSON (asking Claude to 'respond in JSON' via instructions)

dimensions.name: Schema compliance
dimensions.a: 100% guaranteed — constrained decoding makes invalid tokens impossible
dimensions.b: Best-effort — Claude usually complies but can produce invalid JSON, miss fields, or use wrong types

dimensions.name: Error handling needed
dimensions.a: None for schema violations. Only check stop_reason for truncation (max_tokens).
dimensions.b: Need try/catch on JSON.parse, field validation, retry logic for malformed output

dimensions.name: Latency
dimensions.a: Small overhead on first request with a new schema (schema compilation). Cached afterward.
dimensions.b: No extra latency, but retries on failure add unpredictable delay

dimensions.name: Flexibility
dimensions.a: Schema must follow supported JSON Schema subset (no optional fields, limited patterns)
dimensions.b: Any shape you can describe in English — more flexible but less reliable
